questions.question: What is a block of code in Java? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: A block of code is a group of statements enclosed within curly brackets {}.
Blocks of code are used to organize and structure the program's logic.
Example: Method block, Conditional block, Loop block

questions.question: Describe the different types of blocks of code in Java. Provide examples for each type.
keyPoints: Method block: defines the body of a method.
Example: public static void printHello() { // code }
Conditional block: executes code based on a condition.
Example: if (x > 10) { // code }
Loop block: executes code repeatedly.
Example: for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++) { // code }

questions.question: What are the best practices for using blocks of code in Java?
keyPoints: Use meaningful variable names and comments to make the code readable.
Keep the block of code concise and focused on a single task.
Use indentation and whitespace to make the code visually appealing.

questions.question: Explain the importance of indentation, whitespace, and curly brackets in blocks of code.
keyPoints: Indentation and whitespace make the code visually appealing and easier to read.
Curly brackets {} are used to enclose a block of code.
Missing or mismatched curly brackets can lead to errors.
Incorrect indentation or whitespace can make the code harder to read.

questions.question: How are blocks of code used in real-world applications? Provide examples.
keyPoints: Blocks of code are used in web development to create reusable functions and modules.
Blocks of code are used in mobile app development to handle user input and events.
Blocks of code are used in game development to create game loops and handle user interactions.
Example: A login system in a web application uses a conditional block to check user credentials.
